Scenario.title: Email Account Compromise via Phishing Attack
Scenario.overview: Communications Simple Using phishing techniques against internet-facing assets to compromise email accounts and disrupt communication channels.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, OS User Account, Web Server, Database Server, Network Device
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Social Engineering, Web, Command Line, Remote Services, Physical Interaction

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: An adversary uses deceptive emails to trick recipients into providing login credentials or clicking malicious links, specifically targeting accessible points within the organization's digital footprint.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-9: Buffer Overflow in Local Command-Line Utilities
stage.description: After obtaining legitimate credentials, the attacker executes system commands remotely or locally to run unauthorized processes, potentially leveraging vulnerabilities in command interfaces to further compromise the environment.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-194: Fake the Source of Data
stage.description: The compromised process involves executing PowerShell scripts to alter email messages, impersonate senders, or exfiltrate sensitive information, thereby disrupting normal communication functions and achieving the desired impact.

Scenario.summary: A straightforward attack campaign targeting communication systems through phishing, followed by command execution and email manipulation to cause disruption.
